How to Create
Irresistible Sales
Messages for ANY
Business
Module 2: Finding Your USP and
Creating Your Elevator Chat
With Dan Kennedy and John Carlton
www.gkic.com
www.getwsodownload.com

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 4
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Finding Your USP and Creating Your Elevator Chat
Dan Kennedy: John Carlton and I have known each other for a very long time – a
mutual respect society. We both basically approach selling and copywriting with
the same sort of “killer take all” attitude.
We view it as a blood sport. We like it. We like the thrill of the kill. We believe it is
an honorable thing to do, assuming you are selling something honorable.
No-one is more adept at what John is going to talk
to you about here – the finding of the big idea, the
finding of the magic hook, the thing that sets what
you are doing apart from everything else…
sometimes called “Unique Selling Proposition” but
more than that.
So you are in for a treat.
My good friend John Carlton now has you in his
very capable hands.
John Carlton: Thanks Dan. Much appreciated. What I'm going to do, I am not
going to give you theory. Most of you have been to events before, and you go
home. What's the first thing that happens in the week after you go to an event?
You forget everything you learned, right? Because a lot of it is theory and stuff.
So I want to make sure that you cement a lot of stuff in. So I'm going to give you
some exercises, I'll get to that in just a second.
Classic Salesmanship
I will explain to you in just a minute who I am and how I got to this point of
being on the stage here speaking and why I have a little bit of credibility in this
field.
No-one is more
adept than John at
finding the magic
hook that sets what
you are doing apart
from everything else.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 5
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
But first I want to ask how many people would like to learn how to sell without
selling in their business?
Okay, grow up!
It’s not going to happen.
Classic salesmanship is the stuff that started working back when the first
caveman traded up to a cave with a better view in exchange for a slab of
mastodon beef to a salesman who knew how to sell.
This stuff doesn't change much. I call it “classic salesmanship” and it has nothing
to do with “hype.” It has nothing to do with being overbearing and you don't
need to wear the plaid jacket of the used car salesman. You can be who you are.
How many people have an unethical product that they're selling, that they're
ashamed of?
All right good… a couple of people in the back, the
smart ones!
The thing is a lot of people feel shame about
salesmanship. They do associate it with the used
car salesman with the plaid jacket and all of that.
So how many people are uneasy about asking
somebody to buy what you have?
Be honest. Most of you are a little hesitant to do it
or you wouldn't be in this seminar. You'd be relaxing on a beach somewhere.
You'd be filthy rich.
The thing is, if you have an ethical, good product that fixes the problems that
your prospect has, then shame on you if you don't do everything you can in your
power to make sure that he understands that what you have is what he needs,
that you are the person he needs to deal with and he needs to do it right now.
If you have a product
that fixes problems
your prospect has,
shame on you if you
don't do everything
you can to ensure he
understands that.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 6
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
That's where classic salesmanship comes in.
Who is John Carlton?
So who the heck is John Carlton and why should you listen to anything I have to
say?
My story is interesting in that I started out as a “clueless slacker”…. one of the
original clueless slackers.
I was 32 years old before I figured out what was going on. I'd been broke for
most of my adult life until I figured this stuff out. I got fired from every normal
job I ever had, that's serious, including the one mowing the lawn for the next
door neighbor. I was a slacker before they even had the word “slacker.”
The ultimate wake-up call for me was that I lost my job, my girlfriend and my
place to live all within a two-month period.
That was when I was about 32, I think. I wound up living in my car for about six
months.
One day I woke up on a friend's couch down in
San Diego and it dawned on me that, if my life
was going to change, I was the guy who was
going to have to effect that change.
Nobody was going to help me. There was going
to be no magic. I'd given it 32 years waiting for
the magic.
For the next 30 years, I made every mistake possible. I became an advertising
expert before I clearly understood what being an advertising expert entailed but
I figured it out really fast and I needed to get as hip as possible as fast as
possible.
So I took a speed reading course all within this year of making this realization
that I needed to get busy.
It dawned on me
that, if my life was
going to change, I
was the guy who was
going to have to
effect that change.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 7
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
I took a speed reading course, went to the Torrance Municipal Library and read
everything in the Dewey Decimal System from I think 600 to 820, which
includes sales, marketing, writing, business, all of that stuff and just plowed
through the entire decks of the library.
I did that because I was going to be a freelance writer and I was going to go out
into the world of Los Angeles advertising agencies and I figured I needed that
education to bring me up to speed with what everybody else was doing.
Do you know what it actually did?
It put me light years ahead of the first agency owner I met. These guys were
clueless. Even more clueless than me.
Because when I went through
that stack, I found the books
that were out of print like
Claude Hopkins, Robert Collier;
the books that when I later met
Gary Halbert, Jay Abraham, Joe
Sugarman and a lot of the very famous guys – and mentored with a lot of them,
wrote with them and partnered with some of them – these were the books that
we held in common.
Claude Hopkins wrote in the 1920s, Robert Collier in the ‘50s and ‘60s. These
are books that are out of print and nobody in advertising, even to this day at an
agency, pays any attention to the old stuff. They want the new stuff. They want
the hot stuff. Me, I went for the old stuff.
I started interviewing classic streetwise salesmen, the guys who actually were
selling eye-to-eye and, if they didn't make a sale, they probably weren't going to
eat that night. I wanted to learn about that.
So what I did for the next 30 years is I went out, very proud because I knew all
this stuff, but I still made every mistake possible.
30 Freakin’ Years…
…making every mistake possible…
…fixing things until they worked like crazy…
…and taking detailed notes along the way.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 8
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
I continued to fix things after making the mistakes and, this is the important
thing, I took detailed notes along the way.
So when I decided to step out and be a guru about 12 years ago, I had a deep
library of notes that I could tap to be able to teach other people how to do it.
I had been breaking this process down all along the way. This is a process based
in reality because, again, I made the mistakes, then I corrected the mistakes.
So this is all real world stuff.
What I’m Going to Teach (and Not Teach)
I'm going to teach you about 1/10 of 1% of what I have to teach you but I'm
going to teach you something.
I'm going to teach you three drop dead simple exercises. This will actually clear
some things up.
 One, is a very quick one. I want to make sure you understand what you're
really selling.
 Number two is what I'll talk about in this session, which is the elevator
pitch or your USP.
This is the key to everything good that will happen
in your business for the rest of your life.
 And then, in a later session, I will tell you how to write your first damn
good ad in nine minutes.
I'm not going to teach you today how to write. How many people are a little
afraid of writing, a little fearful of sitting down, facing the blank page? Be honest
now. A good chunk of the room.
How many people have suffered writer's block? Okay. Writer's block does not
exist. I will tell you right now. Writer's block is just being unprepared when you
sit down.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 9
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
So don't worry about writer's block. Don't worry about any of this. Nothing I'm
telling you has anything to do with actually sitting down and writing.
By the time you sit down to actually write, after these exercises I do with you,
you will have the ad pretty much already done.
I'm not going to teach you how to be creative.
This isn't being creative. This is about
salesmanship. And of course I'm not going to tell
you how to sell from the stage because I suck at
it. I'm shy. I'm reserved. I'm an introvert. I love vampire. I usually get up around
noon.
Tomorrow I'm speaking at 8:00. That's 5:00 am my time. That's when I usually
go to bed. I may have to stay up all night to be able to make it tomorrow.
Exercise #1: What Are You Selling?
Exercise number one, what are you selling?
Action Step: Define What You Are Selling
What are you selling?
Audience answers:
 Experience
 Solutions to problems
 Relationships
 Return on investment
 Transformation
 Entertainment
 More money
Writer's block is just
being unprepared
when you sit down.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 10
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
It's not the thing that you're selling.
It's the RESULT.
You're not selling relationships. You're selling a better life.
You're not selling more money. You're selling a better life.
A lot of people get confused about what it is you're actually selling.
If you go into writing an ad thinking you're going to tell people how to have a
relationship, that ain't gonna work because that's not what they want.
That's how they may verbalize it. What they really want is the better life that
they imagine at that point comes with having a better relationship.
This is it. This is the first lesson.
This is what a lot of people pay me thousands of dollars to sit down and get
harangued about.
I like to make people cry when I do consultations. Then I know I've broken
through and gotten to the essence of this.
A lot of people get very invested in what they're selling. They think they're
selling a five part course and a series of books and a seminar and all this stuff
and no, not at all.
It all boils down to some very basic stuff. What are the results in your prospect's
life? How has his life changed? The benefits.
Exercise #2: The Elevator Chat
Okay this one I'm going to actually have you work at.
There’s a lot of different versions of the elevator chat. I use it because here's
how you have to think about your USP.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 11
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
First of all most people know USP from the Rosser Reeves definition, which was
a 1950s ad guy on Madison Avenue who wrote slogans for soap and beer and
stuff like that.
The original USP was called the Unique Selling Proposition and he tried to
differentiate a cigarette from another cigarette. Most of that stuff isn't going to
be relevant for you doing direct response. But the concept is still the same.
The way I use USP is I talk about positioning yourself
in the market uniquely in order to sell.
It's different. It's not a slogan. It's positioning yourself.
You can position yourself so that you may be listened to; you may be respected
but you won't sell at all.
You may position yourself in a way where you're just like everybody else. You're
just one of the choices out there. You're not standing out.
So you want to keep all this in mind.
The elevator chat comes in when you imagine you're on the sixth floor of a
building and you get on an elevator.
You're not sure if the person standing next to you is a prospect or not but just
assume they are and they turn to you and they
say, "What do you do?"
The doors close and the elevator comes down.
Can you describe who you are and why you're
important – if they would be a hot prospect, if
they only knew what you had and who you were
and why it was so important?
Can you do it in six floors?
Can you describe
who you are and
why you're
important?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 12
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Most marketers can't. And I'm going to change that today.
Here's the elevator chat template:
We help _________ do ___________ even if _________.
 We help this group of
people do this benefit.
Remember the difference
between a feature and a
benefit. A feature would
be the heft, weight, size of
the thing. The benefit is
what that stuff does for
you – what the emotional,
physical, financial result
of it is
 Better, cheaper, faster,
easier – some kind of quantifier that makes you unique
 Even if, and this is important, the worst case believable scenario.
Here's a couple of examples.
I help small business owners and entrepreneurs…
create their first killer conversations that bring in the Big Bucks…
even if they flunked English or are scared to death of “writing.”
That's kind of like what I do.
We help golfers with huge handicaps…
to immediately start hitting long, straight, gorgeous tee shots and score so
low they win all the money from their buddies…
Elevator Chat Template
We help ___________________ …
… do _____________________ …
… even if __________________
We help (this group of people) …
… do (this BENEFIT) …
… (better, cheaper, faster, easier)…
… even if (worst case believable scenario)
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 13
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
even if you've been slicing, hooking, skulling, hacking and putting like a
baboon your entire life.
We're on the fifth floor. We're still going down. What have I established at this
point? Just about everything that's going to bring that guy, if this is what fulfills
his needs in life. He's already interested, okay?
Here are some more examples:
We help under-staffed biz owners grossing one-to-ten million a year… [very
specific]
to pay the least amount of taxes legally owed each year…
without adding a minute of extra work to your day…
even if your bookkeeping is a total disaster area right now.
We help broke people buried in debt…
to get out of debt within a short time…
and have more money in your pocket without taking on another job or
borrowing.
Works even if you have no understanding of finances or economics
whatsoever.
We help overweight people…
to lose 10 to 20 pounds of body fat within one month…
without ever feeling hungry or deprived or sweaty from exercise.
Works even if you've failed with every diet you've ever tried.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 14
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Action Step: Create Your Own Elevator Chat
I'm going to give you just a couple of minutes to follow the template and create
your own elevator chat.
Do it fast; it's okay to make mistakes but if you don't do this, you won't get it
down. So start now.
Your Elevator Chat
We help _________ do ___________ even if _________.
I like to test people in the brainstorm that I give. I have a very expensive
brainstorm group – it meets four times a year and we like to do the thing,
everybody does it in the room. It's doors closed
and we have them tell us what their elevator
speech is.
90% of even experienced marketers fail.
I'm doing this exercise first because, when I get to
the “How to write your first damn good ad in nine minutes,” this is the essence
of what you will be doing. You nail this and almost everything you write will be
a lot easier – e-mails, ads, speeches.
You nail this and
almost everything
you write will be a
lot easier.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 15
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Audience Examples and Feedback
Example #1
Audience: We help overweight housewives who want to feel better, gain
more confidence, energy and enthusiasm even if they are eating Cheetos on
the couch and can barely get off the couch to get another bag of chips.
John: Okay you're lying there but that's the idea. Now do you have a product
that actually does this?
It's a transformational product in terms of wellness and they would stop
eating the chips because of the information that we're giving them.
That's an excellent first draft to get going but you haven't really nailed it. You
haven't really got down to the essence of this.
When Dan talked in the first session about doing two drafts what he meant was
do a ton of prep and then do a draft and then do a brush up draft and kick it out
the door. I do do a lot of drafts when I do things.
So when I work on this, first of all I don't have a certain number of drafts I do. I
do it until it's right. You know it's right when it can be explained to a stranger as
you go down the elevator, for example, in a very short period of time.
Remember when you're doing this that the person you're talking to in the
elevator, do they like you yet? Do they even care what you have? When they find
out that you have something to sell, their first
response is going to be to get out of the elevator.
So you’ve got to be thinking about that.
Let's call it the grinder or the gauntlet. Run it
through the “so what” gauntlet. That's a famous old copywriter thing. If at any
point it makes them say, “Yeah…so what?”
Run it through the
“so what” gauntlet.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 16
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
If you're talking to somebody who is in your target market, then you want to
make sure that you're speaking the language as Dan talked about earlier – the
“vocabulary”.
That's an excellent way to do it. Start learning to talk.
You look like you've never had an extra pound on you in your life. So you may
have to get this information from someone else. But make sure you're in the
target of what they do and remember that elevator.
In fact you should really test this in the elevator. A lot of people say, "What do
you do?" And they'll go, "Oh well," and they start mumbling and going around
and that's how their ads are, too.
By the time the elevator opens, the person has no idea what you do. You're not
going on. This is the way to get interest and get
in.
The “even if” part is an obstacle obliterator.
So it's not the Cheetos. It's probably “failing at
diets.”
I’ve been in the diet market before. I don't do it anymore but I've been in it
before and they don't want to sweat. They don't want to exercise. There's a lot
of things they don’t want to do.
There's a lot of things they do want to do such as not have to worry about it or
not have to spend very much time. They want magic.
But you have to address all that.
Example #2
Audience: We help people who jointly own real estate separate their
interests in the real estate even if they're unmarried, signed no break up
The “even if” part is
an obstacle
obliterator.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 17
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
agreement, hate each other's guts or end up in court suing each other ‘til
death do us part.
John: Okay. That's not bad. You’re a lawyer, right? I'm left with more questions,
though.
But if this is my target market they're like “yeah I get it.”
Okay that's not bad. When you have something like being a lawyer and it's going
to come in and it might spoil the relationship or it might tarnish how you're
talking to them, I like to come out with it really, really fast, “For anyone who's
afraid of lawyers or anyone who thinks that lawyers are out to get them or
screw them just for the money or don't care about them.” Something along that
line.
Think of the objections that the person will have and
think of those points in the elevator when
the person actually jumps a little bit.
“What? I'm in a divorce right now. My wife is taking me for everything I have.”
What's he going to ask? Go through that scenario.
The way this thing works is when it's believable in a face-to-face-in-an-elevator
scenario. Not bad. Good job.
Example #3
Audience: InfoMarketingSystem.com helps authors, speakers, coaches and
entrepreneurs install an online sales vortex in their business. It sucks in
prospects and automatically converts anonymous website visitors into
lifelong customers and evangelists so you can eliminate all your technical
challenges, make more money and work less even if you don't…
John: Ding, ding, ding, ding, ding…
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 18
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Too long?
Not bad. Better to over-write and work your way back than try to nail it on the
first trip. But I lost you like halfway through.
I kind of got the idea you help me with a website and blah, blah, blah. So think
about you don't care about the guy who's not a prospect in the elevator. If he's
not a prospect, you don't care.
But if he is a prospect, think about the difference between hot and warm and
cold prospects.
Hot prospects are ready to go and as soon as they
find out you’ve got a solution the check’s out and
cash exchanges hands. The cold prospect is still
in the market but needs to be convinced.
So run it through all of that. Yours might work
with a hot one but with a cold one he's going to get turned off.
In the grand world of helping people with websites and stuff, you sound kind of
generic. There ain't nothing special. But good first effort. Excellent.
Example #4
Audience: We help hobbyists with video cameras edit video that
communicates powerfully even if they can't even spell iMovie.
John: Are you just working with Macs?
No.
It's real generic. I think “what are the problems?” for example, and this can help
everybody.
The micro mimics the macro. When you help one person with something,
everybody should be able to get the idea.
Think about the
difference between
hot, warm and cold
prospects.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 19
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
So what is the problem that I have with trying to do my own video stuff? Am I
professional, an amateur? Who are you going after? What's your target market?
The idea is to go after people who want to be able to edit video that
actually makes people feel something.
Do they have any money? Are you making money at this?
Yes. Well actually not yet. Not yet on this.
Okay, you need to define your market. You need to think of who that guy is,
what he's doing now.
When you're talking about target markets, the number one
thing that market has to have is money.
You marketers joke around, what you really want is stupid people, slow learners
with lots of money. That's a perfect market.
Very few of those exist but they do exist and, as you work your way back from
that, you want to stop when you get to the point of “eager to learn, no money.”
After you make your millions you can go back and help those people for free.
My blog is for free, for example. I have eight years of free archives. I share it
because I don't need to make the money anymore. But I had to get to that point
first.
So you’ve got to be targeted. It's like if you're trying to make money with video
you don't understand the secrets that we routinely teach people in a very short
period of time. It's great. “We love working with rookies the best because they
learn the fastest,” something along like that.
Example #5
Audience: We help small business retail and restaurant owners frustrated
with the lack of customers in this economy to use one simple step to easily
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 20
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
get their customers to come back repeatedly paying full price, even if they
have no time or education or budget for advertising.
John: Okay. It's not bad. What is the one step?
It's a way to gather the customer data.
Okay so you're collecting data and e-mailing them or sending them postcards or
something like that?
Right. Marketing messages.
I'm not a restaurant owner but I figured that out halfway through your talk. So
it's not unique. I can Google “help retain customers for my restaurant” and get
pages and pages and pages of offers like that.
So think about how to make that unique. And since I don't know your business, I
don't know quite how you're doing that.
But what is it that sets you apart from everything else? The “even if” is
probably… what does the average restaurant owner want to do next halfway
through his first day after the restaurant's open and bright and shiny people
have come in and ordered and things are happening?
Sell the business and retire.
It's the same for a doctor. A doctor goes through however many years of medical
school to get his license, hangs his shingle, sees his first patient and, next thing,
he wants to retire. He's done.
So when you're talking about restaurant owners, you're not in their head yet.
That's what you need to do when you think about positioning yourself uniquely
in this crowded market, how do you do that?
With restaurant owners and everything else, you better understand what they
are. They are probably fried, they've been burned on multiple things. They're
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 21
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
probably still buying Yellow Page ads that aren't working. They don't
understand Yelp. All of these things.
They feel lost, neglected, burned. So when you get that stuff, start making notes.
 Who's been burned?
 How have they been burned?
 What are the pain points that they have?
 What are the fears that they have?
The two things that most ads work on to sell
are Fear and Greed.
The big funnel of marketing just goes right down to that. So they're either afraid
of something or they're really looking to make a lot of money.
And these are good people, bad people. It doesn't matter. That's business. That's
capitalism.
Restaurant owners, it sounds to me like you haven't spent a lot of time with
them or, if you have, a lot of restaurant owners are just numb to what's been
going on because it's a devastating business to be in if it's not working.
The worst thing about restaurants, of course, is that you can be working one
month and then die off the next month. And you don't even know why. So it's a
horrific business to be in and it's the number one business that people start and
the number one to go broke after it’s about two years the average restaurant
lasts.
I'm pointing out ways you can make yourself unique in the “even if” part – “even
if you've been burned.”
Even if they've been burned by 50% off coupons?
Yes. And you name the stuff. You get specific. You start there.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 22
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Example #6
Audience: We help newbie surfers to ride their first wave within 60
minutes of their first lesson, even if they have the balance of a newborn
baby.
John: I don't like your newborn baby but you're close. I'm not sure about the
market but isn't there another word for the newbie surfer? I mean if you're
talking to surfers. I'm not sure there's a market of people who want to surf and
aren't reading surf mags or looking at surf sites.
Well it's mostly people that have never surfed, on vacation, their families,
that sort of thing.
So you're kind of like the scuba outfits in Central America that give you a half
hour lesson and toss you in the ocean with scuba gear?
Pretty much. Two hour lesson, throw you in and…
All right you can go a lot stronger with that headline then. We take guys who are
scared to death of the ocean, who have never been able to body surf before and
teach you how to stand up on a board and enjoy the wonders of surfing almost
like a pro within 15 minutes and we'll take you out there and do a blah, blah,
even if you trip over yourself walking down the hall late at night.
Example #7
Audience: We help lightweight grapplers make larger, stronger opponents
say “uncle” and get to the next belt faster in Brazilian Jiu-jitsu even if
they're not particularly athletic, experienced or talented.
John: This is Gracie?
Yeah, Gracie style.
So you run a studio?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 23
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
I run a studio and do info-products specifically in the Brazilian Jiu-jitsu
lightweight niche.
How are you doing right now? How's this working out?
We just doubled our space in the academy – 4,500 square feet. I’ve only
been taking the business seriously for a year and a half. I just got into the
online stuff and did my first small launch. I just threw all that dough into
consulting and doing another bigger launch in January.
Okay good. Be careful about mentioning Jiu-jitsu and things like this. Be specific
– “Gracie Jiu-jitsu, if you've ever wanted to X…” whatever that does, as opposed
to, “Learn how to…” what the pure benefits are.
So they don't need to know at this point that it's Gracie Jiu-jitsu. That’s the
“thing.” What they need is the benefit. I've been in the martial arts business a lot.
So I know how to talk to these guys.
The first headline I had was “How to Humiliate a Black Belt and Make Him Putty
in Your Hands.” That was the ad. I was appealing to a lot of those guys and we
ran the ad in Black Belt and things like that because we knew from research that
half the people in Black Belt have never trained, have no idea what to do.
They're kind of “looky loos” but they want come in. And it's the same in golf.
I did my famous golf ad, “The Amazing Secrets of a One-Legged Golfer that Will
Help You Slash Strokes and Hit Longer Drives….”
The idea was they had a client who actually learned this from a one-legged
golfer. But what we did is, we ran three and four page ads in Golf Digest and Golf
Magazine, the biggest golf magazines in the biz back in the 90s.
The editors at the magazine mocked us. They said, "Really we don't want to take
your money. This is not going to work." And it was solid copy for three pages,
the first one. It was a right hand page, double truck ad. Later we added one little
photo but that was it. Solid copy.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 24
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
They said, "This is not going to work. You guys are just wasting your money."
These are $70,000 ad outlays.
By the time we went back for the fifth insertion, they tried things like moving it
to the back of the mag. They tried to hide it. They were embarrassed by it
because they didn't understand what we understood and what I understood…
The average golfer can't break 100 to save his life.
So we started running ads. The next one we ran was “How Does this Overweight
Golfer Crippled by Arthritis and Unable to do a Complete Back Swing Still
Humiliate PGA Pros on a Regular Basis in the Underground Golfing World?”
I liked the word “humiliate” back then. I was using it quite a bit but I was
speaking to the average reader.
The guy who didn't think Tiger Woods could teach him anything. The guy who
knew he was never going to be on the Nike tour let alone the PGA tour. So you
know who you're talking to and you target them.
Example #8
Audience: We give people back youthful sexuality, focus and weight
control in less time than it takes to watch a weekly sitcom, without reliance
on multiple prescription medications and
doctor visits.
John: Okay that's a mouthful. If you had a perfect
prospect that may work. When I’m up against…
kind of dancing around the subject… sometimes I
write the ad, the headline or the elevator chat
that I could never use in polite company and then
I back up from there.
So I write the ad that could never be printed. The client would never let it run
and I swear and I use slang and I just get it out.
Sometimes I write
the ad, headline or
elevator chat that I
could never use in
polite company.

I just do a number on the thing. So you're talking about guys who want youthful,
teenage hormone levels to overcome the syndromes of… I won't say it in public.
I can't even say what's going through my head.
But you start there and then you back it up and sometimes in the elevator you
can say that and that's what he wants to hear.
He doesn't want to hear this stuff, "Yeah, yeah I've heard of Viagra. I've heard of
Yohimbe root and I've heard of all this stuff. I don't want to do any of that."
So how are you unique in that? How can you do that because there's a lot of
people making those promises?
Well I founded the Hormonal Blueprint System and…
That's the thing. What does that thing do? How fast?
Well in a very short time because…
Will it work tonight?
No.
Will it work next weekend?
Well we could say that, in less than 60 days…
60 days, okay. That's what we call an objection. I don't want 60 days. I will go
buy the inferior crap that promises it'll work in an hour, before I'll do something
like that.
We're putting you back to your youthful hormonal blueprint. In other
words when you were 20 you had a hormone…
I'm making fun of you but I figure you can take it because you're bigger than I
am. What does your perfect prospect look like? Who is he?
Well it's not he, it's both men and women.

What does he look like? The “he” part of that men and women.
Anywhere from 30 to 60. They're overweight. They're fatigued.
Are you selling a million dollars of this a month? A million a year?
A year.
You're selling a million a year?
Yes.
Okay. Well that's good. That's way too wide a thing. I think I can double that for
you, if you get more specific.
I mean we're making people feel as good as they did when they were 20.
How are you reaching them? Through what media?
TV commercial.
How much are you paying for that commercial? I've never seen this.
Okay well it's in my local market. We pay $5,000 a month.
$5,000 for a late-night five minute commercial, two minute one?
No we're doing The Doctors, we do Oz and The Ellen Show. We only do
three commercials a day.
Sweet deal. Okay. And you've gotten them to endorse it?
No, no. Those are the shows, that's the timing of the actual commercial.
All right. Well there's no arguing if you're doing it but I'm telling you that's wide;
if you start to narrow that, you should test. That's your control what you're
making a million a year with.
You wanted to say something but you said because of reasons…, what are
you wanting to hear or what would define that?

I'm about to turn 61 and if you said that to me in an elevator I'd be yawning
before you're done. I just don't care about that.
Now maybe I'm not your target market. But I think you're missing a whole lot of
stuff because when you start talking about hormonal…, and I've written for
these markets. It's just the more specific you can get, the more you can narrow it
down.
So encapsulate what you would say would be the buzz word that would
capture that?
Well I think, talk to the people. What's the main thing they come away with?
What is the main benefit?
Energy, focus, the relationships are better.
Why are their relationships better?
Well because they're in balance.

Well I did though. I said, should I just…
You really didn't. You danced around it.
Take all the big, fancy 30 cent words you're using and find the five cent word
equivalent and test that.
So for youthful sexuality what would your word be?
Screw like a teenager!
I was trying not to go there, guys.
Example #9
Audience: I've got two. One I've been using for years.
John: Pick one.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 28
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Eight words is the second one. I'll do it fast. Really quickly.
The one I came up with tonight using your template is, we help people
protect their most priceless possessions even if they live in a high crime
area with a system so simple to use, a child could operate it.
What's the system?
Security.
What kind of security?
Electronic.
That's where I kind of thought you were going. So that's a crowded market.
You're up against millions of dollars and people saying that they're the one to do
it.
You trip and fall. You go, "Help I can't get up." And they somehow hear it. And
they are, "Oh I got help on the line." You're up against a lot of unethical
marketing stuff. So you’ve got to get past more than that.
So, protecting my valuables... I get it. I hate being a homeowner but I am a
homeowner. You know what I want to happen to an intruder who comes after
my valuables? So think about that. I don't want them killed but I want them to
hurt real bad and not be able to reproduce or not be able to use the stuff.
Can I give you the eight word one that works?
Yeah.
We stop bad things happening to good people.
Yep and that's a cliché. You're going to have to work on that because you may
come across other versions of that in different markets but that ain't bad.
Clichés work because clichés zoom in the side door
and head right for a spot.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 29
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
But you’ve got to be careful because sometimes they don't think the cliché is
true. But clichés can work.
Example #10
Audience: I help online marketers to get free targeted traffic from
YouTube even if they have never uploaded a single video before.
John: Free traffic from YouTube.
Correct.
It's not that unique. What is it that you do?
I can say “clients” or “sales” instead of “traffic.”
So I put a YouTube up and you show me how to what? Get the names, get them
to sign into my list and…
How to get your viewers to your e-mail list.
So it's lead generation?
Yes.
Okay. Not bad. But you don't stand out. That's a really crowded market. The
“YouTube” thing doesn't make you unique. Lead
generation really doesn't make you unique.
So what is unique about this? It's like who are
you going after? Who's going to do this?
A lot of people make the mistake you make of
thinking “online” is a market. It's not. Online is
just another description of a medium for
marketers to be in. So “who” in the online world is going to benefit the most?
And you start small.
Online is not a
market. It's just
another description
of a medium for
marketers to be in.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 30
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
Another tactic a lot of you are misusing is you're trying to sell three, four things
at once.
If you're having any trouble at all or you're stumbling, sell one thing. You can
always sell more things to people who become clients or customers or sign up
on your list. So sell one thing.
You could sell one thing to… pick any sub-stratum of the market and just try that
out and target it, especially if you're using AdWords – you're pulling not
pushing. Are you reaching people through AdWords?
No.
How are you reaching? Organic search?
No, I bring my videos on top of the search results and that's how I get the
traffic. So I don't need to pay Google for that.
All right. You can start to target that a little bit more.
This simple tactic can change a lot of things and it's like a
headline – it often will become your first big headline.
How Deals Gets Closed
Now I just want to remind you how deals get closed. This is a free lesson. It's
very short. It takes seconds.
What you want your prospect to
come away with from your first
conversation with him is
“rational” reasons and “lizard
brain” reasons.
 Rational Reasons: The
rational reasons is what
they will tell their wife when they buy; what they'll tell the neighbor who
How Deals Get Closed
Rational Reasons:
What they tell others
Lizard Brain Reasons:
Why they really buy
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 32 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 31
© 2014 GKIC Module 2
always tries to make them feel bad; how they will justify it to their
drinking buddies who think they're an idiot.
Those are the rational reasons. I bought the Porsche because it's a great
investment in a vehicle. One of the finest machines made.
 Lizard Brain Reasons: And then there are lizard brain reasons and you
have to get to that, too. You have to support them.
Why does a guy buy a Porsche? Midlife crisis number 12.
But you have to tap into that.
It's greed and fear and a sense of power and a sense of all kinds of thing but you
have to approach both.
It’s All About
It's all about grabbing their attention amidst the chaos.
Unique. The unique part of positioning yourself.
Luring them into your world because you can convince somebody.
It's easy to convince someone to say, "That's a great product you've got there.
That's really good and I could
probably use it. I think sometime
down the road I just may buy
that."
That's what we call getting them
up on the fence. Professionals
knock them off the fence.
So you lure them into your world
and you help them realize that
this is what they need and want and you are the person they want to deal with.
That pretty much is the essence of old school classic salesmanship.
It’s All About
Grabbing their ATTENTION amidst the chaos…
Luring them into YOUR WORLD…
And helping them realize that THIS is what they
NEED and WANT…
And YOU ARE THE PERSON they want to deal
with.
www.getwsodownload.com
